OBJECTIONS TO PLTF'S EVIDENCE FILED BY DEFENDANT HANSON, BRIDGETT, MARCUS, VLAHOS & RUDY, LLP

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HANSON, BRIDGETT, MARCUS, VLAHOS & RUDY, L.L.P., RICHARD STRATTON, JAMES HOLDEN, AND WALTER SCHNEIDER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 301, JUDGE BUSCH HAS RECUSED HIMSELF FROM THIS CASE). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS CITE NO CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY ON POINT FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT AS A MATTER OF PUBLIC POLICY, THE MOTION MUST BE GRANTED. DEFENDANTS CONTENTIONS THAT SUMMARY JUDGMENT SHOULD BE GRANTED, BASED UPON IN PARI DELICTO AND UNCLEAN HANDS, ARE REALLY JUST THE PUBLIC POLICY ARGUMENT RESTATED. MOREOVER, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE VALUE, IF ANY, OF THE FREEMAN STOCK, AND IF IT HAD VALUE, WHETHER THE USE OF SUCH EVIDENCE WAS A VIABLE TRIAL STRATEGY. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE UNDERLYING JUDGMENT HAS BEEN SATISFIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED BECAUSE IT IS NOT CLEAR THAT THE BANKRUPTCY COURT DECIDED THE IDENTICAL ISSUE. COURT'S RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE SIGNED AND FILED THIS DATE; COPIES ARE GIVEN TO BOTH COUNSEL. MR. FRASSETTO IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR OCT-22-2007 VACATED.

Defendant Hanson, Bridgett, Marcus, Vlahos & Rudy, Llp, Richard Stratton, James Holden, Walter Schneider'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From Dept. 301, Judge Busch Has Recused Himself From This Case.)
Off Calendar Sep-14-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS HANSON, BRIDGETT, MARCUS, VLAHOS & RUDY, L.L.P., RICHARD STRATTON, JAMES HOLDEN, AND WALTER SCHNEIDER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 301, JUDGE BUSCH HAS RECUSED HIMSELF FROM THIS CASE).
APPEARANCES: PAUL A. FRASSETTO, ESQUIRE (ZITRIN & FRASETTO, L.L.P., 415.354.2712) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MICHAEL P. BRADLEY, ESQUIRE (MURPHY PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY, 415.788.1900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS CITE NO CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY ON POINT FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT AS A MATTER OF PUBLIC POLICY, THE MOTION MUST BE GRANTED. DEFENDANTS CONTENTIONS THAT SUMMARY JUDGMENT SHOULD BE GRANTED, BASED UPON IN PARI DELICTO AND UNCLEAN HANDS, ARE REALLY JUST THE PUBLIC POLICY ARGUMENT RESTATED. MOREOVER, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE VALUE, IF ANY, OF THE FREEMAN STOCK, AND IF IT HAD VALUE, WHETHER THE USE OF SUCH EVIDENCE WAS A VIABLE TRIAL STRATEGY. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE UNDERLYING JUDGMENT HAS BEEN SATISFIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED BECAUSE IT IS NOT CLEAR THAT THE BANKRUPTCY COURT DECIDED THE IDENTICAL ISSUE. COURT'S RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE SIGNED AND FILED THIS DATE; COPIES ARE GIVEN TO BOTH COUNSEL. MR. FRASSETTO IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/LB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hanson, Bridget, Marcus, Vlahos & Rudy's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories 4, 20, and 27 is granted. The response and supplemental responses to Nos. 4 and 27 are improper. The supplemental response to No. 20 is incomplete as it does not identify documents with particularity, as it states documents include certain categories, and as it reserves the right to submit additional documents. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
